trends.name: Wilfa Uniform grinder hype train
trends.buzz: The Wilfa Uniform is trending hard as a budget flat-burr grinder that punches above its price point. Coffee Reddit is calling it the best entry-level option for pour-over enthusiasts who want consistent grinds without spending $300+.
trends.angle: Perfect for anyone leveling up from a blade grinder or entry hand grinder — you don't need to break the bank to unlock better cups at home.

trends.name: Fellow Opus grinder firmware updates & modding
trends.buzz: Fellow Opus owners are sharing grind-setting sweet spots and discussing burr swaps to get even better pour-over performance. The sub-$200 grinder continues to dominate beginner-to-intermediate conversations.
trends.angle: The Opus proves you can start with a solid grinder and grow into it — tweaking settings is part of the fun, not a barrier.

trends.name: Orea V4 brewer buzz
trends.buzz: The Orea V4 pour-over brewer is getting fresh attention for its flat-bed design and forgiving brew window. Fans love how it handles everything from light naturals to darker roasts with minimal fuss.
trends.angle: Flat-bed brewers like the Orea are all about consistency and room to experiment — no need to nail a perfect spiral pour every single time.

trends.name: Timemore Sculptor grinders gaining traction
trends.buzz: Timemore's Sculptor line (064, 078) is earning praise as a mid-tier grinder option with excellent pour-over clarity. The 64mm flat burrs and solid build quality are winning over home brewers looking for value.
trends.angle: You don't need the most hyped grinder to make incredible coffee — the Sculptor shows that thoughtful design and good burrs go a long way.

trends.name: Iced coffee season recipes flooding feeds
trends.buzz: With summer in full swing, Japanese iced coffee and flash-brew recipes are dominating specialty coffee communities. Brewers are sharing ratio tweaks and ice-to-coffee formulas for V60, Kalita, and Origami drippers.
trends.angle: Iced pour-over isn't just hot coffee poured over ice — it's a chance to play with ratios and discover how your favorite beans taste cold.

trends.name: April Brewer plastic vs glass debates
trends.buzz: The April Brewer continues to spark discussion about whether the plastic or glass version is worth it, and whether flat-bottom geometry really changes the cup. Fans swear by its clean, tea-like extractions.
trends.angle: Brewer choice is personal — if you're curious about flat-bottom or the April hype, try it, but your trusty V60 isn't wrong either.
